Morgan Fitzgerald

    I am a second-year Ph.D. student in Dr. Bradley Voytek’s lab. My interests revolve around unraveling the relationship between peripheral and cortical dynamics. Specifically, my focus lies in investigating the impact of cardiac signals on scalp electroencephalography (EEG). Read more about my ongoing projects.

    In addition to my research, I am deeply committed to advancing open science and diversity within the neuroscientific community. You can read more about my outreach efforts here.

    BACKGROUND

    • Raised in Boulder, CO and San Diego, CA.

    • Graduated from UC Santa Barbara in May of 2020.

      • At UCSB I worked with Dr. Scott Grafton’s and Dr. Emily Goard-Jacob’s.
      • My senior thesis probed the role of endogenous fluctuations in female steroid hormones on cerebellar functional dynamics, employing functional magnetic resonance (fMRI) techniques.
        • Read the publication here.

    • I moved back to San Diego in 2020 to join Dr. Matthew Panizzon’s lab at UC San Diego.
      • My independent project focused on the effect of the early life hormone milieu on migraine risk.
        • Read the publication here.

    • I joined the Neuroscience Ph.D. program at UC San Diego in August of 2022 and subsequently joined Dr. Bradley Voytek’s lab in June of 2023.

    PERSONAL LIFE

    I enjoy maintaining my college tennis skills by playing in local clinics. Beyond the tennis court, I find joy in photography, baking, and learning to surf.
